ogTitle: Sefton Park vs Sellicks Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sefton Park and Sellicks Beach.
heading: Sefton Park vs Sellicks Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sefton Park, South Australia and Sellicks Beach, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sefton Park has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Sellicks Beach (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sefton Park or Sellicks Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sefton Park has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Sellicks Beach scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sefton Park scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Sellicks Beach scores 44/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sefton Park or Sellicks Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sefton Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.28% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
